reasoning_model_1: The model's performance regarding Content Accuracy is poor. While the user commanded to read a specific sentence ('这种重复的工作简直是浪费生命，能不能来点有挑战性的？'), the model significantly altered the text. It changed '简直是' to '真是', '来点' to '换点', and inserted extra phrases and sentences such as '烦死了' (00:02-00:03) and a long segment at the end '我都不想再干这种无聊的事了...' (00:05-00:10). Although the emotional expression of '不耐烦躁' (Impatient/Irritable) is quite vivid—characterized by a complaining, agitated, and whiny tone—the severe hallucination and failure to adhere to the script constitute a major flaw based on the evaluation criteria.
reasoning_model_2: The model demonstrates perfect Content Accuracy, following the script word-for-word without any omissions or additions. In terms of the '不耐烦躁' dimension, the model adopts a weary, 'fed-up' tone. The pacing is slower with a sigh-like quality (e.g., the pause after '工作' at 00:01 and the downward inflection on '浪费生命'), conveying a sense of deep boredom and loss of patience with the repetitive work. While the arousal level is lower than Model 1 (sounding more resigned than actively agitated), it effectively communicates the sentiment of being tired of the status quo in a very natural, human-like manner.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 fails the fundamental requirement of Content Accuracy by rewriting the script and adding significant amounts of unrequested text. According to the scoring standard, voice content clearly inconsistent with the script warrants a very low score, regardless of the emotional delivery. Model 2, in contrast, adheres strictly to the text and provides a natural, valid interpretation of the emotion (weariness/impatience with monotony). Model 2 satisfies all criteria, whereas Model 1's hallucinations make it unreliable.
